title: Arni Repository Setup & Infrastructure
description:
## Overview
Establish professional Rust project structure and infrastructure for the Arni multi-database library. Set up workspace organization, build tooling, testing framework, CI/CD, and developer experience tools to support TDD workflow and make it easy for developers to contribute.

## Goals
- Professional Cargo workspace structure (library + CLI)
- Automated build and development scripts
- Comprehensive testing infrastructure (unit, integration, TDD)
- CI/CD pipeline with quality gates
- Complete developer documentation and onboarding
- Consistent code quality tooling (rustfmt, clippy)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Cargo workspace configured with arni lib and arni CLI crates
- [ ] Build scripts operational (build.sh, dev.sh, test.sh)
- [ ] Testing framework with TDD workflow documented
- [ ] CI/CD pipeline running on PR and merge
- [ ] Developer guide and CONTRIBUTING.md complete
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks configured
- [ ] Code quality tools integrated (rustfmt, clippy, cargo-deny)
- [ ] All scripts executable and documented

## Scope
**In Scope**:
- Cargo workspace structure (multi-crate layout)
- Build automation scripts
- Testing infrastructure and TDD workflow
- CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions)
- Code quality tooling
- Developer documentation
- Local development setup
- Pre-commit hooks

**Out of Scope**:
- Actual database adapter implementation
- Performance benchmarking infrastructure (future)
- Release automation (future)
- Docker/container setup (future)

## Features
| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| Project Foundation | Cargo workspace, directory structure, core project files |
| Build & Dev Tooling | Scripts for building, testing, watching, formatting |
| Testing Infrastructure | TDD setup, test organization, coverage tools |
| CI/CD Pipeline | GitHub Actions, quality gates, automated checks |
| Developer Experience | Documentation, onboarding, contribution guidelines |

---
*Living document - updates as discovery progresses*

title: Arni Multi-Database Data Library
description:
## Overview
Build a comprehensive Rust data library (arni) mirroring the Python skidbladnir library. Provides unified, adapter-based access to multiple database systems (Oracle, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, MSSQL Server, DuckDB) with CRUD operations, metadata queries, and Polars DataFrame integration. Includes both library and CLI components.

## Goals
- Unified adapter interface for multiple database systems
- Type-safe CRUD operations returning Polars DataFrames
- Comprehensive metadata query capabilities (tables, columns, schemas)
- Configuration file-based connection management
- Professional CLI tool for database operations
- Feature parity with Python skidbladnir library
- Test-driven development with ≥80% coverage

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 5 database adapters implemented and tested
- [ ] Common DbAdapter trait defined and implemented
- [ ] CRUD operations work across all adapters
- [ ] Metadata queries functional for all adapters
- [ ] Results returned as Polars DataFrames
- [ ] Configuration system supports all DB types
- [ ] CLI tool with full CRUD and metadata commands
- [ ] Integration tests pass for all adapters
- [ ] Test coverage ≥80%
- [ ] API documentation complete

## Scope
**In Scope:**
- Core adapter framework (traits, types, errors)
- Database adapters: PostgreSQL, MSSQL Server, Oracle, DuckDB, MongoDB
- CRUD operations: Create (INSERT), Read (SELECT), Update, Delete
- Object creation: Tables, collections, indexes
- Metadata queries: List tables/collections, describe schemas, column info
- Polars DataFrame integration for all result sets
- Configuration file system (YAML/TOML)
- CLI tool with subcommands (connect, query, metadata, etc.)
- Comprehensive error handling
- Unit and integration testing

**Out of Scope:**
- Database migrations/schema versioning
- Connection pooling optimization (v1.0)
- ORM functionality
- Database-specific advanced features (stored procedures, triggers)
- Web API/REST interface
- Real-time streaming

## Features
| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| Core Adapter Framework | DbAdapter trait, Connection trait, error handling, DataFrame wrapper |
| PostgreSQL Adapter | Full PostgreSQL support with metadata and CRUD |
| MSSQL Server Adapter | SQL Server support via tiberius crate |
| Oracle Adapter | Oracle database support via oracle crate |
| DuckDB Adapter | Embedded analytics database support |
| MongoDB Adapter | NoSQL document database support |
| CRUD Operations | Unified Create, Read, Update, Delete across adapters |
| Metadata Queries | Schema introspection and database information |
| Configuration System | YAML/TOML-based connection profiles |
| CLI Tool | Command-line interface for all operations |
| Testing & Integration | Comprehensive test suite with fixtures |

---
*Living document - updates as discovery progresses*

title: Project Foundation
description:
## Overview
Establish core Cargo workspace structure with library and CLI crates. Set up directory organization, fundamental project files (Cargo.toml, .gitignore, etc.), and define the basic module structure for the arni library.

## Scope
**In Scope:**
- Cargo workspace with 2 crates: arni (lib) and arni-cli (bin)
- Directory structure (src/, tests/, examples/, docs/)
- Core Cargo.toml with workspace dependencies
- Basic module structure (traits, adapters, config, errors)
- CLAUDE.md for TDD configuration
- .gitignore for Rust projects
- LICENSE and README updates

**Out of Scope:**
- Actual adapter implementations
- CLI command implementations
- Database connection code

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cargo workspace builds successfully
- [ ] arni lib crate created with module structure
- [ ] arni-cli bin crate created
- [ ] cargo build passes
- [ ] cargo test passes (even with empty tests)
- [ ] Project structure matches best practices

title: Developer Experience
description:
## Overview
Create comprehensive developer documentation and contribution guidelines to make it easy for developers to onboard and contribute to the project.

## Scope
**In Scope:**
- CONTRIBUTING.md with workflow
- Development setup guide
- Architecture documentation
- Code style guidelines
- PR and review process
- Issue templates
- VS Code/RustRover settings recommendations

**Out of Scope:**
- API documentation (generated via rustdoc)
- User-facing documentation
- Tutorial/example projects

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CONTRIBUTING.md complete with examples
- [ ] Development setup documented
- [ ] Architecture overview documented
- [ ] Code review checklist created
- [ ] Issue/PR templates configured
- [ ] Editor settings documented

title: Create CONTRIBUTING.md
description:
**INCOMING**: All workflows established
**OUTGOING**: Contribution guide

## Description
Write comprehensive CONTRIBUTING.md with setup instructions, development workflow, PR process, and code style guidelines.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CONTRIBUTING.md created
- [ ] Setup steps documented
- [ ] Development workflow explained
- [ ] PR process described
- [ ] Code review checklist included
- [ ] Example contribution walkthrough

title: Write architecture documentation
description:
**INCOMING**: Core traits defined
**OUTGOING**: Architecture docs

## Description
Create docs/architecture.md explaining adapter pattern, trait design, and module organization.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Architecture overview documented
- [ ] Trait hierarchy explained
- [ ] Adapter pattern described
- [ ] Module organization documented
- [ ] Diagrams included (text-based)

title: Setup issue/PR templates
description:
**OUTGOING**: GitHub templates

## Description
Create .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/ and .github/pull_request_template.md for consistent issue and PR formatting.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Bug report template created
- [ ] Feature request template created
- [ ] PR template created
- [ ] Templates include all required sections
- [ ] Labels configured

title: Document editor setup
description:
**OUTGOING**: Editor configuration guide

## Description
Create docs/editor-setup.md with VS Code and RustRover recommendations, extensions, and settings.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] VS Code extensions listed
- [ ] RustRover settings documented
- [ ] Recommended settings.json provided
- [ ] Debugging configuration explained
- [ ] Rust analyzer configuration documented

title: Build & Development Tooling
description:
## Overview
Create build automation scripts and development tooling to streamline the development workflow. Includes scripts for building, testing, formatting, linting, and file watching for rapid development.

## Scope
**In Scope:**
- Build scripts (build.sh, clean.sh)
- Development scripts (dev.sh with cargo-watch)
- Testing scripts (test.sh with coverage)
- Format and lint scripts (fmt.sh, clippy.sh)
- Makefile for common commands
- cargo-make task definitions

**Out of Scope:**
- Release builds and packaging
- Docker containerization
- Deployment scripts

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All scripts executable and documented
- [ ] Scripts work on macOS and Linux
- [ ] Makefile targets covering all common operations
- [ ] cargo-watch integrated for live reloading
- [ ] Scripts include error handling
- [ ] Usage documentation in README

title: Testing Infrastructure
description:
## Overview
Set up comprehensive testing infrastructure following TDD principles. Configure test organization, coverage tools, and document the TDD workflow for all contributors.

## Scope
**In Scope:**
- Unit test structure and organization
- Integration test setup
- Test coverage with cargo-tarpaulin
- TDD workflow documentation
- Test fixtures and helpers
- CLAUDE.md TDD configuration
- Mock/test database configurations

**Out of Scope:**
- Performance/benchmark tests
- Fuzzing infrastructure
- Property-based testing (future)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unit tests runnable via cargo test
- [ ] Integration tests in tests/ directory
- [ ] Coverage reports generated
- [ ] TDD workflow documented in CLAUDE.md
- [ ] Test coverage target ≥80% configured
- [ ] Example tests demonstrating patterns

title: CI/CD Pipeline
description:
## Overview
Implement CI/CD pipeline using GitHub Actions with quality gates for automated testing, linting, and code quality checks on every PR and commit.

## Scope
**In Scope:**
- GitHub Actions workflow for CI
- Quality gates (tests, clippy, rustfmt)
- Code coverage reporting
- Multi-platform testing (Linux, macOS, Windows)
- Dependency security scanning (cargo-deny)
- Pre-commit hooks configuration

**Out of Scope:**
- CD/deployment automation
- Release workflow
- Performance regression testing

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GitHub Actions workflow file created
- [ ] Tests run on all commits to main and PRs
- [ ] Clippy and rustfmt checks enforced
- [ ] Coverage reports posted to PRs
- [ ] Security scanning operational
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks configured

title: Initialize Cargo workspace
description:
**OUTGOING**: Working Cargo workspace configuration

## Description
Initialize Cargo workspace with two crates: arni (library) and arni-cli (binary). Configure workspace-level dependencies and shared settings.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cargo.toml workspace file created
- [ ] arni lib crate initialized in crates/arni/
- [ ] arni-cli bin crate initialized in crates/arni-cli/
- [ ] cargo build succeeds for workspace
- [ ] Shared dependencies defined at workspace level

title: Initialize Cargo workspace
description:
**OUTGOING**: Working Cargo workspace configuration

## Description
Initialize Cargo workspace with two crates: arni (library) and arni-cli (binary). Configure workspace-level dependencies and shared settings.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cargo.toml workspace file created
- [ ] arni lib crate initialized in crates/arni/
- [ ] arni-cli bin crate initialized in crates/arni-cli/
- [ ] cargo build succeeds for workspace
- [ ] Shared dependencies defined at workspace level

title: Define core traits and types
description:
**INCOMING**: Workspace structure
**OUTGOING**: Core trait definitions

## Description
Define the fundamental traits for database adapters: DbAdapter trait, Connection trait, and core types (DataFrame wrapper, Result types, Error types).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] DbAdapter trait defined with CRUD methods
- [ ] Connection trait defined
- [ ] Error type hierarchy created
- [ ] DataFrame wrapper type defined
- [ ] All traits compile with dummy implementations

title: Implement configuration system
description:
**INCOMING**: Core traits defined
**OUTGOING**: Configuration loader

## Description
Create configuration file loader supporting YAML/TOML for database connections. Support multiple connection profiles.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Config struct defined (supports all DB types)
- [ ] YAML parsing working
- [ ] TOML parsing working
- [ ] Example config files created
- [ ] Config validation implemented

title: Setup directory structure
description:
**INCOMING**: Workspace initialized
**OUTGOING**: Complete directory tree

## Description
Create directory structure: src/adapters/, src/config/, src/traits/, tests/integration/, examples/, docs/.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All directories created
- [ ] mod.rs files in place
- [ ] Directory structure documented in README
- [ ] .gitkeep files where needed

title: Create CLAUDE.md TDD config
description:
**OUTGOING**: TDD workflow documented

## Description
Create CLAUDE.md following Rust TDD best practices template with cargo test commands, coverage targets, and code style rules.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CLAUDE.md created with Rust TDD template
- [ ] Test commands documented
- [ ] Coverage targets defined (≥80%)
- [ ] Code style rules specified (rustfmt, clippy)
- [ ] TDD workflow documented

title: Build & Development Tooling
description:
## Overview
Create build automation scripts and development tooling to streamline the development workflow. Includes scripts for building, testing, formatting, linting, and file watching for rapid development.

## Scope
**In Scope:**
- Build scripts (build.sh, clean.sh)
- Development scripts (dev.sh with cargo-watch)
- Testing scripts (test.sh with coverage)
- Format and lint scripts (fmt.sh, clippy.sh)
- Makefile for common commands
- cargo-make task definitions

**Out of Scope:**
- Release builds and packaging
- Docker containerization
- Deployment scripts

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All scripts executable and documented
- [ ] Scripts work on macOS and Linux
- [ ] Makefile targets covering all common operations
- [ ] cargo-watch integrated for live reloading
- [ ] Scripts include error handling
- [ ] Usage documentation in README

title: Create build scripts
description:
**INCOMING**: Cargo workspace
**OUTGOING**: Build automation scripts

## Description
Create shell scripts for building: build.sh (debug/release), clean.sh, check.sh. Include error handling and platform detection.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] build.sh created with debug/release modes
- [ ] clean.sh removes target/ directory
- [ ] check.sh runs cargo check
- [ ] Scripts executable (chmod +x)
- [ ] Scripts work on macOS and Linux
- [ ] Documentation in scripts/

title: Create development scripts
description:
**INCOMING**: Build scripts
**OUTGOING**: Development workflow scripts

## Description
Create dev.sh with cargo-watch for live reloading, fmt.sh for formatting, clippy.sh for linting.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] dev.sh with cargo-watch integration
- [ ] fmt.sh runs rustfmt on all code
- [ ] clippy.sh runs clippy with deny warnings
- [ ] All scripts handle errors gracefully
- [ ] Usage examples in scripts/ README

title: Create testing scripts
description:
**INCOMING**: Test infrastructure
**OUTGOING**: Test automation scripts

## Description
Create test.sh for running tests with options, coverage.sh for generating coverage reports using cargo-tarpaulin.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] test.sh with unit/integration/all options
- [ ] coverage.sh generates HTML reports
- [ ] Scripts support CI environment detection
- [ ] Minimum coverage threshold configurable
- [ ] Coverage reports in target/coverage/

title: Create Makefile
description:
**INCOMING**: All scripts created
**OUTGOING**: Unified build interface

## Description
Create Makefile with targets for common tasks: build, test, fmt, clippy, clean, coverage, dev.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Makefile with all common targets
- [ ] help target lists all commands
- [ ] Targets call scripts/ files
- [ ] Phony targets declared
- [ ] Documentation in Makefile comments

title: Testing Infrastructure
description:
## Overview
Set up comprehensive testing infrastructure following TDD principles. Configure test organization, coverage tools, and document the TDD workflow for all contributors.

## Scope
**In Scope:**
- Unit test structure and organization
- Integration test setup
- Test coverage with cargo-tarpaulin
- TDD workflow documentation
- Test fixtures and helpers
- CLAUDE.md TDD configuration
- Mock/test database configurations

**Out of Scope:**
- Performance/benchmark tests
- Fuzzing infrastructure
- Property-based testing (future)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unit tests runnable via cargo test
- [ ] Integration tests in tests/ directory
- [ ] Coverage reports generated
- [ ] TDD workflow documented in CLAUDE.md
- [ ] Test coverage target ≥80% configured
- [ ] Example tests demonstrating patterns

title: Setup unit test structure
description:
**INCOMING**: Core traits defined
**OUTGOING**: Unit test framework

## Description
Set up unit test organization with test modules, create example tests, and establish test patterns.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test modules created alongside source
- [ ] Example unit tests for traits
- [ ] Test helpers module created
- [ ] Mock implementations for testing
- [ ] Documentation for test patterns

title: Setup integration tests
description:
**INCOMING**: Unit tests configured
**OUTGOING**: Integration test framework

## Description
Create tests/ directory with integration tests, test fixtures, and test database configurations.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/ directory with integration tests
- [ ] Test fixtures for each adapter
- [ ] Test database connection configs
- [ ] Integration test examples
- [ ] Test data cleanup utilities

title: Configure coverage tooling
description:
**INCOMING**: Tests running
**OUTGOING**: Coverage reporting

## Description
Set up cargo-tarpaulin for coverage, configure coverage targets (≥80%), and create coverage reporting scripts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] cargo-tarpaulin configured
- [ ] Coverage threshold set to 80%
- [ ] HTML coverage reports generated
- [ ] Coverage badges configuration
- [ ] CI coverage integration ready

title: CI/CD Pipeline
description:
## Overview
Implement CI/CD pipeline using GitHub Actions with quality gates for automated testing, linting, and code quality checks on every PR and commit.

## Scope
**In Scope:**
- GitHub Actions workflow for CI
- Quality gates (tests, clippy, rustfmt)
- Code coverage reporting
- Multi-platform testing (Linux, macOS, Windows)
- Dependency security scanning (cargo-deny)
- Pre-commit hooks configuration

**Out of Scope:**
- CD/deployment automation
- Release workflow
- Performance regression testing

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GitHub Actions workflow file created
- [ ] Tests run on all commits to main and PRs
- [ ] Clippy and rustfmt checks enforced
- [ ] Coverage reports posted to PRs
- [ ] Security scanning operational
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks configured

title: Create GitHub Actions workflow
description:
**INCOMING**: Test scripts ready
**OUTGOING**: CI pipeline configured

## Description
Create .github/workflows/ci.yml with jobs for build, test, clippy, and rustfmt across multiple platforms.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ci.yml workflow created
- [ ] Tests run on Linux, macOS, Windows
- [ ] Clippy check with deny warnings
- [ ] rustfmt check enforced
- [ ] Build matrix for Rust stable/nightly
- [ ] Workflow triggers on PR and push to main

title: Configure coverage reporting
description:
**INCOMING**: CI workflow running
**OUTGOING**: Coverage in CI

## Description
Add coverage job to CI workflow, configure codecov/coveralls integration, post coverage to PRs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Coverage job in ci.yml
- [ ] Codecov integration configured
- [ ] Coverage comments on PRs
- [ ] Coverage badges in README
- [ ] Coverage fails if below 80%

title: Setup security scanning
description:
**INCOMING**: CI workflow
**OUTGOING**: Security checks automated

## Description
Add cargo-deny for dependency scanning, configure cargo-audit for vulnerability checks.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] cargo-deny configuration created
- [ ] Security scan job in CI
- [ ] cargo-audit scheduled scans
- [ ] Dependabot configured
- [ ] Security policy documented

title: Configure pre-commit hooks
description:
**INCOMING**: Dev scripts ready
**OUTGOING**: Local quality gates

## Description
Set up pre-commit hooks for rustfmt, clippy, and tests using pre-commit framework or git hooks.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] pre-commit config created
- [ ] Format check on commit
- [ ] Clippy check on commit
- [ ] Test run on commit (optional)
- [ ] Hook installation documented
